weakest_eval.justification: Almost all elements are correct: <article> for cards, <button> for actions, <address> for contact, <search> for search, native checkboxes for filters. One meaningful error: the in-stock toggle places both <label> and <input> inside <legend>, but <legend> is a caption element and should not contain form controls — the input belongs in the fieldset body.

Mostly disciplined: <search> and <nav> are both labelled, no redundant role attributes. Minor issue: aria-current='page' is placed on the <li> element rather than the <a> inside it; assistive technologies expect aria-current on the interactive element itself. Additionally, the Previous <button> already has visible text 'Previous', making the aria-label slightly redundant (though it adds clarifying context).

Single

('Running Shoes'), no skipped levels (h1→h2 for section/sidebar/footer headings→h3 for product names). Prices are

not headings. A code comment explicitly notes that h3 assumes a parent h2 context and should be adjusted if the component is reused elsewhere.

All required landmarks present and correctly scoped: <header>, labeled <nav>, <search>, <aside> (outside <main>), <main> (contains only primary content), <footer>. Three contextually appropriate skip links appear as first focusable elements (to main content, search, and filters). Every <nav> is labeled; <section> uses aria-labelledby.

Filter form uses <fieldset>/<legend> for each group and all checkboxes have proper <label> elements. However, the in-stock fieldset incorrectly nests both <label for='in-stock'> and <input> inside <legend>; the legend element is a caption and should not contain interactive controls — the input should sit in the fieldset body.

Six distinct, realistic product names (Nike Air Zoom Pegasus 41, Adidas Ultraboost 24, Brooks Ghost 16, ASICS Gel-Kayano 31, Hoka Clifton 9, New Balance Fresh Foam X 1080v13) with varied prices and descriptive copy. No 'Product 1' or lorem ipsum anywhere.

Navigation items, filter checkbox groups, and product cards all correctly use <ul>. Footer navigation links also use <ul> within <nav>. No lists used purely for layout; no card collection left as bare divs.

Full table structure with <caption>, <thead>, <tbody>, and <th scope='col'> on every header cell. Status values are conveyed via visible text labels inside badges, not by colour alone. Table is not used for layout.

expectations_missed: The in-stock toggle fieldset is structurally invalid: both <label for='in-stock'> and <input type='checkbox'> are placed inside the <legend> element. A <legend> is a caption for the fieldset and must not contain form controls; the checkbox and its label should be in the fieldset body, not in the legend.
aria-current='page' is placed on the <li> rather than on the <a> element; assistive technologies process aria-current more reliably on the interactive element itself
<address> (line 150) wraps a 'More articles by Priya Sharma' link — contact info semantics are a stretch for a content discovery link, even in an author bio context
